message:
perf(btree): throttle cursor-op cancellation checks (OPT-3)

Profile showed `<Cx>::checkpoint` 2.75% + `<asupersync::Cx>::checkpoint`
2.03% = 4.78% self-time on the INSERT hot path. The full checkpoint
walks the e-process oracle + the asupersync native-cx shim (which
calls `clock_gettime` on every invocation). Cursor traversals fire
`observe_cursor_cancellation` thousands of times per INSERT.

Semantics preserved: the cheap `cancel_requested: AtomicBool` load
still fires every invocation, so cancellation latency stays bounded
by the time between the requester's Release store and the next
cursor op — NOT by the throttle interval. Only the e-process /
native-cx path gets throttled to 1/64 invocations.

Thread-local counter so multiple cursors on the same thread share
the throttle (they're all making the same kind of progress anyway).

Expected savings: drops the ~4.8% of self-time to roughly
4.8/64 ≈ 0.07%. At 14.9us per UPDATE, that's ~720ns reclaimed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
